prerequisites: An Azure Blob Storage account with the account name (azure_blob_storage_account_name).
The name of the Blob container to read from (azure_blob_storage_container_name).
Credentials for one of the supported authentication methods, with at least Storage Blob Data Reader permissions on the container and blobs.
At least one stream definition (streams): a name, a file format (CSV, JSONL, Parquet, Avro, or Document file types), and a glob/path pattern for matching files.

authMethods.name: Storage Account Key
authMethods.summary: Open Source only. Authenticate with the storage account access key, supplied via credentials.azure_blob_storage_account_key. View and copy a key in the Azure portal under your storage account at Security + networking > Access keys (use Show keys, then copy the Key value).

authMethods.name: Client Credentials (service principal)
authMethods.summary: Open Source only. Authenticate with a Microsoft Entra app registration using credentials.app_tenant_id (Directory/Tenant ID), credentials.app_client_id (Application/Client ID), and credentials.app_client_secret (Client Secret). The service principal must be granted the Storage Blob Data Reader role on the storage account.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Cloud only. Authorize through Azure login; the connector stores credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.refresh_token, and credentials.tenant_id. The authorizing identity needs the Storage Blob Data Reader role on the storage account.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the storage account and permissions
setupSteps.body: Identify the Azure Blob Storage account name and the container you want to replicate. Ensure the identity or credential you will use has at least the Storage Blob Data Reader role on the storage account so it can read containers and blobs.

setupSteps.title: Obtain credentials for your chosen auth method
setupSteps.body: For Storage Account Key auth, open the storage account in the Azure portal, go to Security + networking > Access keys, select Show keys, and copy a Key value. For Client Credentials auth, register an application in Microsoft Entra ID and record its Directory (Tenant) ID, Application (Client) ID, and a Client Secret. For OAuth 2.0 (Cloud), you authorize interactively through Azure login during source setup.

setupSteps.title: Configure the source connection
setupSteps.body: Set azure_blob_storage_account_name and azure_blob_storage_container_name, then select an authentication method and enter its credentials. Optionally set azure_blob_storage_endpoint to override the endpoint domain name and start_date to only replicate files modified on or after that date.

setupSteps.title: Define streams
setupSteps.body: Add one or more entries to streams. For each stream provide a name, choose the file format (CSV, JSONL, Parquet, Avro, or Document file types), and set a glob/path pattern (for example ** for all files or **/*.csv) to select which blobs to ingest. Optionally provide an input schema to enforce column types.

sources.title: Airbyte: Azure Blob Storage source connector
